channel.title: Starmer Watch — Pressure Index
channel.description: Composite pressure index, top news, and market signals for Keir Starmer's leadership.

item.title: Pressure Index 55/100 (elevated)
item.description: Public pressure bloc is above the modeled 81 MP trigger. Pressure Index 55/100 (elevated). Polymarket's top Starmer-exit signal is 9.5%.

item.title: Independent UK Politics: Boris Johnson described Theresa May as a ‘giant Yucca tree under whose shade everything dies’
item.description: Boris Johnson described Theresa May as a ‘giant Yucca tree under whose shade everything dies’

item.title: Guardian (Open Platform): Minister dismisses ‘two-tier justice’ claim in light of Henry Nowak tragedy as ‘slur’ on police – UK politics live
item.description: Minister dismisses ‘two-tier justice’ claim in light of Henry Nowak tragedy as ‘slur’ on police – UK politics live

item.title: Independent UK Politics: Darren Jones told Mandelson he was ‘so sorry’ after sacking over Epstein ties

item.title: Independent UK Politics: Mandelson files: The key takeaways from latest release of disgraced peer’s messages with Starmer’s top ministers

item.title: Independent UK Politics: The real reason Farage has seized on the murder of Henry Nowak
item.description: The real reason Farage has seized on the murder of Henry Nowak

item.title: The Guardian: Starmer says Farage ‘dodging questions’ about £5m gift from crypto billionaire - The Guardian

item.title: The Guardian: MPs raise doubts over missing Mandelson vetting documents - The Guardian
item.description: MPs raise doubts over missing Mandelson vetting documents - The Guardian

item.title: The Guardian: Starmer’s chief secretary consoled Mandelson after dismissal as US ambassador, undisclosed texts show - The Guardian

item.title: ITVX: Starmer condemns 'disgraceful' violent protests over Henry Nowak murder - ITVX

item.title: BBC Politics: Victims of Jeffrey Epstein ignored by government, says former minister
item.description: Victims of Jeffrey Epstein ignored by government, says former minister
